headings.usage: Use for all page titles, dashboard card headers, and investor-facing high-level metrics.

body.usage: Use for all standard text, descriptions, and UI controls.

mono.usage: CRITICAL: Use for all biological sequences (FASTA), molecular coordinates (PDB), file hashes, and specific data table metrics to ensure perfect digit alignment.

typography.general_rules: Never use Inter, Roboto, or generic sans for headings. Apply tracking-tighter to main headings. Make overlines/labels uppercase, tiny (text-[10px] or text-xs), with wide spacing (tracking-[0.2em]). Install font packages locally to ensure 100% offline functionality.

colors.rules: Follow ARCHETYPE 4: SWISS & HIGH-CONTRAST mixed with the requested rich colors. The UI should feel like a 'Technical Command Center'. To avoid typical standard color looks, ensure the background is always slightly tinted (Slate) rather than pure white/black. DO NOT use dark gradients or purple/pink. Ensure minimum 4.5:1 contrast for text.

visual_enhancers.surface_strategy: GRID BORDERS (The Technical Look). Expose the skeleton. Dashboards should have a border-collapse style layout or sharp 1px borders (border-slate-200 in light mode, border-slate-800 in dark). No muddy drop-shadows. Use flat, solid containers.
visual_enhancers.hover_states: Subtle background tint shifts (e.g., hover:bg-slate-50 or hover:bg-slate-800). Interactive elements should feel highly responsive but not bouncy.
visual_enhancers.branding: For PeptiCascade, build a typographical logo with a Lucide 'Hexagon' icon and 'Manrope' Black text. Ensure it looks authoritative and investor-ready. PDF exports MUST include this branding prominently.

grids.dashboard_mode: High Density Grid Protocol. Base grid: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4. Gap strategy: Tight (gap-4 or gap-6).
grids.bento_rules: Summary widgets: col-span-1. Primary Data (Plotly charts): col-span-2 row-span-2. Feed/List (Job Queue): col-span-1 row-span-2. Every pixel must be utilized. Use h-full on children to stretch.

layouts.navigation: Left sidebar (collapsible for deep data focus). Top bar for global actions (Upload, Search, Settings).
layouts.spacing_system: Generous internal padding for breathing room within cards (p-6). But tight gaps between the cards themselves to maximize data density on the screen.
layouts.panels: Simulation configuration panels (GROMACS, LAMMPS) are complex. They should slide in (Sheet/Drawer) or use focused tabs so users do not lose their context in the dashboard.

motion.rules: Minimalist. Focus on micro-interactions. Use staggered entrance animations for the 2,880 simulation results list to make loading feel fast and powerful.
motion.progress: CRITICAL: Job queue requires smooth, non-jittery progress bars for long simulations to convey stability.

accessibility.contrast: Minimum 4.5:1 for standard text. Test colors thoroughly, especially the Deep Blue against dark mode backgrounds.
accessibility.data_viz: Charts must have tooltips, high contrast lines, and clear legends. Plotly configurations must adapt to light/dark themes.
accessibility.testing_ids: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attribute in kebab-case.

media.icons: Use Lucide React (lucide-react). It matches the sharp, crisp technical requirements perfectly.
media.images: Keep image usage minimal as this is a data-heavy tool. Limit to the abstract backgrounds provided in image_urls for login/empty states.

3d_libraries.primary: plotly.js (via react-plotly.js) for all 2D interactive charts and basic 3D scatter plots.
3d_libraries.molecular: For advanced molecular structures, use a dedicated offline React wrapper like 'molstar' or use Plotly's 3D scatter if specialized offline libraries aren't feasible. The prompt requires 100% offline, so ALL libraries must be installable via npm/yarn without relying on external CDNs.
3d_libraries.rules: DO NOT make external API calls for fonts or libraries.

image_urls.description: Abstract molecular structure with hexagonal rings. Use as a subtle background pattern for the login screen or 'Empty State' visualization lab.
Abstract molecular structure with white and teal spheres. Use for hero banners in investor export reports.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Initialize the project with React, Tailwind, and Shadcn UI. Ensure completely offline operation (bundle fonts locally). Remember you are working with .js/.jsx files, NOT .tsx.

2. Setup Plotly for React (`react-plotly.js`). Customize the Plotly default layout template to match our Light/Dark themes (e.g., set `paper_bgcolor` to transparent, `plot_bgcolor` to transparent, `font.family` to IBM Plex Sans).
3. Build the PeptiCascade Logo: Use `` combined with text `PeptiCascade`. NO placeholder text.
4. Construct the Dashboard Bento Grid to immediately load the 2,880 simulations from JSON. Use virtualization (e.g., react-window) if rendering all results in a list to maintain high performance.

5. Use pure HTML/Tailwind for structural layout and customized Shadcn components for inputs, dropdowns, and dialogs. Ensure strict 1px borders (`border-slate-200`) and remove drop-shadows for the 'Technical Grid' look.
6. Build a comprehensive 'Export Center'. You must implement PDF export using `jspdf` and `html2canvas` (or equivalent offline methods) to generate the branded PDF locally without internet.
7. Simulation configuration panels for GROMACS, LAMMPS, PySCF, Avogadro, SciPy, RDKit should use Shadcn forms with dense layout (IBM Plex Sans, text-sm) to fit complex scientific parameters clearly.
8. Ensure all interactive elements have `data-testid` attributes.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and crisp 1px borders instead of muddy shadows.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements.
You MUST NOT center align the app container. Do not add `.App { text-align: center; }`.
Always use modern button styles with interaction animations. NO EMOJIS.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭 for icons. Use Lucide icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
Use generous spacing where appropriate inside containers, but respect the High Density Grid for data layouts.
Subtle grain textures, custom cursors, and crisp 1px borders separate good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Components MUST use named exports. Pages MUST use default exports.
Use `sonner` for toasts. It is located in `/app/src/components/ui/sonner.jsx` or similar JS file.
GENERAL UI UX DESIGN GUIDELINES: Context Matters: This is a high-density, professional scientific tool aimed at investors. It must balance heavy data presentation with an elegant, expensive feel.
Grid Transparency: Use 1px borders on containers to create a technical 'blueprint' feel. Remove standard card shadows.
Color Intent: Deep Blue (#2563eb) is the authoritative primary color. Emerald Green (#10b981) represents success/sustainability. Amber (#f59e0b) represents running processes/energy.
Data Typography: Always use Monospace (JetBrains Mono) for scientific values, sequences, and exact metrics to ensure vertical alignment of digits.
Frictionless Forms: Complex configurations for tools like GROMACS/LAMMPS must be broken down into logical steps or categorized tabs. Do not overwhelm the user with 50 inputs at once.
Immediate Value: The 2,880 existing results must be accessible immediately upon load. The dashboard should not feel 'empty' on first launch. Show aggregated stats instantly.
